query_text: Plan a 3-day trip for 7 people traveling from St. Louis to Seattle from November 21st to November 23rd, 2024, with a budget of $9,450. Accommodations should allow pets, and the itinerary should include dining options featuring American, French, Mediterranean, and Greek cuisines. Transportation should not involve self-driving.

properties.description: This must-see museum exhibits creative and colorful blown glass sculptures of renowned artist Dale Chihuly. Eight indoor galleries and lush landscaped gardens feature a mix of new and past significant works. They are awe-inspiring whether you visit during the day, or in the evening when the sculptures are lit up. The highlight here is a 100-foot-long glass sculpture suspended in a glass conservatory—one of his largest ever. Located at the base of the iconic Space Needle, you might want to buy a combined ticket for cost savings or get a Seattle City Pass. – Tripadvisor

properties.description: The Pike Place Market has been a defining Seattle icon for more than a century. Considered by many “the soul of Seattle,” the Market spans nine historic acres in the center of downtown where everyday locals and tourists alike shop, visit, eat and discover. Founded in 1907, the Market is one of the oldest and largest continuously operating public markets in the United States and is brought to life by the hundreds of farmers, crafters, small businesses, and residents that call it “home”. For more elbow room, visit on weekday mornings.

properties.description: Located in the Lower Queen Anne neighborhood, this iconic landmark and observation tower was built for the 1962 World’s Fair. Perch on the glass benches known as “Skyrisers” and gaze out of floor-to-ceiling windows, snap a selfie at the outdoor deck, or head upstairs to The Loupe, an indoor lounge featuring the world’s first and only rotating glass floor. Here, you’ll be greeted with 360-degree views of the downtown Seattle skyline, Mount Rainier, Elliot Bay, and the Olympic and Cascade Mountains. For a smooth-sailing experience, it’s best to pre-purchase a timed ticket. Space Needle is also a part of CityPASS. – Tripadvisor

properties.description: Walk the aisle of JFK's Air Force One and climb aboard the Concorde at Seattle’s Museum of Flight. Built around Boeing’s original factory, it is the world’s largest air and space museum. Here, you can learn everything about the history of aviation, from the Wright Brothers and the exploration of Mars, to revolutionary aircrafts and spy planes. You’ll get to barrel-roll a Mustang, land on the moon, soar over Puget Sound in a simulator, and even sit at the controls of the fastest jet on Earth. Certain days of the month have free entry, so check before you go. – Tripadvisor

properties.description: Get a postcard view of downtown Seattle and Elliott Bay against the backdrop of snow-capped Mount Rainier from this beautiful terraced park. You'll also get spectacular views of the Space Needle, a 605-foot-tall observation tower. Take pictures and lounge or have a picnic on the lawn. The best time to visit is during sunrise, or at night to admire the buildings and ferries at the bay glow with lights. Though located in the quiet Queen Anne neighborhood, Kerry Park is less than a mile away from Seattle Center, where you can visit other landmarks like the Museum of Pop Culture. – Tripadvisor

properties.description: The Hiram M. Chittenden Locks (locally known as the Ballard Locks) is a waterway system that connects the saltwater of the Puget Sound to the fresh water of the Ship Canal, which sits about 20 feet above sea level. Boats enter the locks, the gates close and the water rises or falls to the level of the destination waterway. Watching boats pass through the locks, exploring the 7 acres of botanical gardens and looking for fish on the fish ladder is fun and free entertainment that draws locals and tourists alike.

properties.name: Lola
properties.description: Lola offers modern, Greek-influenced cuisine with local ingredients. With menu items like salads, kebabs, lamb chops, eggplant, and cod, you’ll enjoy a bit of Greek food with a Pacific Northwest twist. Located in downtown Seattle, Lola restaurant serves a contemporary twist on the classic favorite Greek fare. It’s a lively eatery by Tom Douglas honoring his wife, Jackie’s grandmother Lola who married Louis a Greek immigrant, both full of life. With our menu, we celebrate Louie's Greek spirit and Lola's feistiness. You can find photos of them both on the walls behind Pope's table.

properties.name: Six Seven Restaurant
properties.description: It's all about cravings at Six Seven Seattle Restaurant. Satisfying the ones you have, and creating sensory experiences that are totally unexpected. At our Seattle restaurant, you'll find a delightful melange of exciting flavors-highlighting the best of Northwestern cuisine and of the area. Our signature Seattle restaurant offers Pacific Northwest Cuisine and breathtaking views. Located inside The Edgewater, our waterfront Seattle restaurant, Six Seven, features seasonally inspired cuisine crafted with only the freshest and finest organic and naturally raised ingredients, procured for you to enjoy in our Executive Chef's new and exciting menus. To complement our Chef's special preparations, our ever-evolving wine selections highlight the finest artisan, boutique and hard to find Washington, Oregon and Northern California wineries. A purposeful selection of allocated wines are sought and paired with ongoing special dinners and promotions to highlight your dining experience.

properties.name: Duke's Seafood South Lake Union
properties.description: South Lake Union Restaurant with waterfront views. Join us at Duke's for a tasty, sustainable lunch, happy hour or dinner from the second story of our waterfront restaurant overlooking South Lake Union. Try our oyster bar, a signature Duketail, or any of our sustainable meat and seafood options – all while enjoying sweeping lakefront views of the stunning Seattle landscape. Take in the sights of the Space Needle, the entrance to the Seattle Locks, and a lake teeming with pleasure boats and seaplanes.

Matt's the Market's dedicated staff is knowledgeable, affable, and seek to create genuine connections and warm memories for every guest.

properties.description: Named as "the definitive steakhouse in the country," El Gaucho's award-winning menu features 28-Day Dry-Aged Niman Ranch All-Natural Certified Angus Beef(R) Brand Prime steaks and custom aged Certified Angus Beef(R) brand tenderloin filets, ribs, pork, poultry and the freshest seafood, all prepared to perfection over an open bed of glowing coals in an exhibition-style kitchen. Our extensive wine list is award-winning, and happy hour menu is widely recognized as one of Seattle's best. El Gaucho is the Northwest's steakhouse legacy focused on old-school, tableside service. We revel in helping guests create a night to remember.
